teaching_card.title: Quadratic Equations (Medium)
teaching_card.body: A quadratic has an x² term. Many factor into (x−r₁)(x−r₂)=0, whose solutions (roots) are r₁ and r₂. The sum of the roots equals −b/a and the product equals c/a, a useful shortcut on the test.
key_points: Set each factor equal to zero to find the roots.
Sum of roots = −b/a; product of roots = c/a.

screens.title: Roots from factors
screens.body: Many quadratics factor into (x − r₁)(x − r₂) = 0. Setting each factor to zero gives the solutions (roots) r₁ and r₂. Watch the signs: a factor (x − 5) has root +5.
key_points: Set each factor equal to zero.
Factor (x − r) has root r (sign flips).

screens.title: Sum and product shortcuts
screens.body: For x² + bx + c, the roots add to −b and multiply to c (for a leading 1). More generally, sum = −b/a and product = c/a — handy when you only need the sum or product, not each root.

screens.title: How many real roots?
screens.body: The discriminant b² − 4ac tells you the number of real solutions without solving: positive -> two, zero -> one (a repeated root), negative -> none. Compute it carefully, signs included.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: x² − 5x + 6 = 0 factors as (x − 2)(x − 3) = 0, so x = 2 or 3 (sum 5, product 6). For x² + x + 4 = 0, the discriminant 1 − 16 = −15 < 0, so there are no real solutions.
